free_text: (alpha,pxn) reactions on natural samarium.
More
details of experiment.
Needeed
information to find the connection between the authors'
understanding of "cumulative cross section" and
generally accepted one.
The ion current was
monitored by the reaction.
The reactions were
used to determine incident energy and to calculate the
alpha-particles flux.
The data for
reactions on aluminum.
.The data for
reactions on copper.
Each stack consisted of several samarium foils with
thickness 18.7-mg/cm**2, purity 99.9%.
Aluminium and copper foils were interleaved in samarium
stack to degrade the beam energy and to monitor the ion
current and maximum incident energy.
The target stack was thick enough to stop the
87-MeV internal alpha beam.
No absolute beam energy determination was
performed in this experiment.  The authors observed
excellent agreement incident beam energy with known
excitation function of monitor reaction.
An information about irradiation and cooling
times is not given.
The total uncertainty, which is produced by:
The uncertainty in the measurement of foil
thickness was 2%.
The error due to energy fluctuations was 0.3%.
The error of incident alpha-particle energy
in each foil of the stack was about 5%.
Estimated uncertainty in the energy for the
region of 20-70 MeV was +-1.5-MeV.
.Equilibrium and pre-equilibrium reaction models.
Tbl.2 from JRN,162,145,1992
Last checking has been done.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents (zeros
were added to 'right side' of data values according to
Tbl.2). Compiler's derived data: DATA(-ERR) deleted;
author's data: MISC(-ERR) -> DATA(-ERR); COMMENT added
in Subents 002-008. Jour.code:ZP/A -> ZP in MONIT-REF.
see COMMENT
/by Compiler/ To get presentation of cr.section in
usual form, someone should multiplying values on the
average atomic mass of target (in this case: ~150.36)
SD: SF8=DAM added to REACTION code.
Compiler's derived data: DATA(-ERR) deleted; author's
data: MISC(-ERR) -> DATA(-ERR). COMMENT added.
see COMMENT
/by Compiler/
1. To get presentation of cr.section in usual form,
someone should multiplying values on the average atomic
mass of target (in this case: ~150.36)
2. Half-life value of Eu-146 is incorrect. According
to ENSDF  t1/2=4.61 days.  The authors suppose, that a
check of half-lives and gamma-rays energies was
carried out.  Therefore a misprint can not be excluded.
3. Half-life of 146-Gd is 48.37 days. Therefore
Eu-146 production due to radioactive decay 146-Gd is
very  small.
/By authors/ Only Sm-144(alpha,n+p) reaction take
place up to 40 MeV of energy.
